subject: Mathematics
chapter: Chapter 1: A Square and a Cube

key_points: A perfect square is obtained by multiplying a number by itself
A perfect cube is obtained by multiplying a number by itself three times
Square numbers follow the pattern n²
Cube numbers follow the pattern n³
Square roots and cube roots help find the original number
formulas: Square of a number → n² = n × n
Cube of a number → n³ = n × n × n
Square root → √a gives the number whose square is a
Cube root → ∛a gives the number whose cube is a
exam_tips: Remember common squares such as 1² to 20²
Remember common cubes such as 1³ to 10³
Check calculations carefully when finding square roots
Do not confuse square numbers with cube numbers
